pages.title: What are jobs?
pages.body:
The cat mafia hands out contracts. You take a job, send cats as your crew, and roll the dice. Win, and your cats come home plus a reward. Lose, and your crew is destroyed.

Each NPC has their own jobs. **Whiskers** is reliable. **Jinx** keeps things low-heat. **Sofia** pays big in cats. **Jeremy** pays in coins. **Lucian Sr** sends you against higher-ranks. Each has trade-offs.

Higher mafia rank unlocks bigger jobs with bigger payouts. At Lv10 (El Patrón), Whiskers offers you **The Big Score** — Wilson's eGirl vault — once per battlepass season.

pages.title: Send Power (SP) and how sending works
pages.body:
**SP (Send Power)** is a per-rarity number that decides how much a cat contributes to a job. It is *not* the same as the cat's trade value — it's a separate game-balance number that compresses the high end on purpose, so sending a stack of Fines stays viable.

**Send Power per rarity:**
```
Fine       1     Brave      18    Mythic    52    Real      115
Nice       2     Rickroll   22    8bit      61    Ultimate  140
Good       3     Reverse    26    Corrupt   71    eGirl     180
Rare       5     Superior   31    Professor 83
Wild       7     Trash      37    Divine    97
Baby       9     Legendary  44
Epic      12
Sus       15
```

**Diminishing returns per rarity.** The more cats of the *same* rarity you stack in one send, the less effective each one becomes. Formula: `effective_SP = base_SP × count^0.75`. The penalty applies to each rarity independently, so **mixing rarities preserves full efficiency**.

```
Count of one rarity → effective multiplier
   1   → 1.00×
   5   → 0.67×
  10   → 0.56×
  50   → 0.38×
 100   → 0.32×
1000   → 0.18×
```
So 100 Fines contribute about 32 SP, not 100. To make a 500-SP wall with pure Fines you'd need ~30,000. Bringing 4 Ultimates + 30 Fines reaches the same wall with no penalty to either rarity (since counts are small).

The send screen shows the effective SP per row, with the raw number in parens when dampening kicked in.

**How a job's odds are decided:**
Each job has a **Difficulty** (a target SP). Your **Ratio** is your crew's total effective SP divided by Difficulty.

```
Ratio 0.25 →  14%  Suicide mission
Ratio 0.50 →  22%  Long shot
Ratio 0.75 →  35%  Risky
Ratio 1.00 →  50%  Even money
Ratio 1.25 →  65%  Decent
Ratio 1.50 →  73%  Good odds
Ratio 2.00 →  82%  Strong
Ratio 2.50 →  90%  Excellent
Ratio 3.00+→  95%  Lock (ceiling)
```
There is **no 100%** — the chance is capped at 95%.

pages.title: The three outcomes
pages.body:
When you commit, the bot rolls **two dice** — the success die and the complication die (independent). The success die lands in one of three bands:

- **Success** — your cats come home, you get the reward.
- **Near-miss** — a narrow 10pp band right above your success threshold. Half your crew survives (random pick by count). No reward. Failure rep.
- **Total failure** — anything beyond the near-miss band. All cats destroyed.

**Near-miss is rare relief, not consolation.** Most failures are total wipes.

The **complication die** can mess with your outcome regardless of how the success die landed — a rival crew can downgrade a hard-won success to a near-miss, an informant can force a near-miss, or you might find a stash and double your reward. See the **Complications** page for the full catalog.

**The roll is final.** Once you click **Send Crew**, the outcome is locked. There is no undo — that's the gamble.

pages.title: Complications
pages.body:
Every commit rolls a **second die**, independent of the success roll. If it hits, a **complication** fires from the per-tier pool. This is what closes the 95% loophole — even a maxed-SP crew can get unlucky on the complication die.

**Base chance by tier:** 5% (T1) · 10% (T2) · 15% (T3) · 25% (T4) · 35% (T5 Big Score).

Modifiers stack: `final = base × (1 + heat_factor) × (1 − rep_discount)`.
- **Heat 31-70**: +10% to the base. **Heat 71+**: +25%.
- **Offerer rep** buys it down: -0.4% per point, capped at **-40%** (about +100 rep with the offerer).

**The catalog** (each event slots into one of three phases):

*Pre-roll (modifies the success die):*
- **Rival crew** — a second SP wall at 40% of difficulty. Fail it and you're downgraded to near-miss regardless of the success roll.
- **Boss arrives** — difficulty ×1.4, success die rerolls against the new wall.

*Post-roll (modifies the outcome or reward):*
- **Cat Police raid** — +30 heat on top of base. Can pinch you if you were close.
- **Double-cross** — offerer skims half your cat reward. Coins untouched.
- **Informant** — force near-miss regardless of how the success die rolled.
- **Easy mark** — reward (coins + cats) doubled. Success only.
- **Found a stash** — bonus 1 cat one rarity above the recipe. Success only.
- **Sloppy target** — replace cat reward with a pack one tier above the recipe default. Success only.

*Aftermath (paid on the next commit):*
- **Witness** — next job's difficulty +20%.
- **Loose end** — +10 heat on the next commit, silently.

**T1 is sweet** — only `easy_mark` is in the pool, so newbies get bonuses, never punishments. **T5 has no aftermath** — Big Score is once per season, no next job to apply to.

The send screen shows your live complication chance. The result screen names the event that fired and the flavor line. Pending aftermath effects show up on the **next** send screen so you're not blindsided.

pages.title: Reputation
pages.body:
Reputation is **per-server, per-NPC**. Every NPC tracks how you've treated them: complete a job *for* them and your rep with them rises; complete a job *against* their faction and your rep with that target tanks. A failed job (near-miss or wipe) costs an additional -2 rep with the offerer.

**Rep gain/loss per tier completed:**
```
Tier 1:  +3 offerer  /  -5 target
Tier 2:  +5 offerer  /  -8 target
Tier 3:  +8 offerer  / -12 target
Tier 4: +12 offerer  / -20 target
T5 Big Score:  +20 Whiskers / -50 Wilson on success
```

**Mechanical effects:**
- **Offerer rep** boosts success chance by **+0.075% per point**, cap **±12%**. Whiskers at +100 = +7.5% success on his jobs.
- **Offerer rep is also complication insurance.** Each point buys down the complication die by 0.4%, capped at -40%. Whiskers at +100 cuts your complication chance from 25% to 15% on his Tier 4 jobs. See the **Complications** page.
- **Target rep (negative)** adds **+0.5% difficulty per point**, cap **+25%**. Wilson at -100 = ×1.25 SP wall on his jobs.
- **Below -25** with an NPC: they **refuse** to offer you work. Their slot rerolls.
- **At or below -75**: target is **hostile**. Heat costs against them drop by 25%.

**Threshold unlocks (Whiskers and Sofia only):**
- **Whiskers ≥ +100** — **Favor.** Once per battlepass season, your next pack-open upgrades by one tier (Wooden → Stone, etc.).
- **Sofia ≥ +100** — **Premium menu.** When she rolls onto your board, she offers her highest-tier-available job.

**General band effects:**
- ≥ +50: NPC's slot weighting increases (you see them more often).
- +50 to -25: normal.
- Rep does **not decay**. It's a record, not a mood.

Run `/rep` to see your standing with everyone.

**NPC identity also drives which perks they drop.** Each NPC has a personality-flavored perk pool — Whiskers leans on pack and crew insurance, Lucian Jr is impulsive and pack-heavy, Jinx hands out catnip-side buffs, Jeremy is all coin perks, Lucian Sr does vendetta/rep stuff, Sofia is the dealer. Higher-tier jobs drop perks more often and from a different sub-pool. See `/perks` to view what's active. Big Score (Whiskers, T5) always drops a capstone perk.

pages.title: Perks
pages.body:
**Mafia favors (job perks)** are a third reward axis on top of coins and cats. They are **previewed on the offer card** — you see what each available job is offering as a bonus *before* you accept. The perk is rolled when the offer is generated, not when you commit, so you can pick between contracts on the bonus as much as the reward.

**Where you see them:**
- On each `/jobs` board card (the line that starts 🎁).
- On the send screen once you've accepted, with concrete strength: "Double Cat (6h)".
- In the public Accept embed posted to the channel — others see what you're chasing.
- On the result screen — "delivers on the promise" if you win, "the bonus walks" if you don't.

**How they drop:**
- Per-tier roll at offer-generation: T1 8%  ·  T2 15%  ·  T3 25%  ·  T4 35%  ·  T5 100% (Big Score).
- Each NPC has a personality-flavored pool (see the Reputation page).
- **Drops are success-only.** Near-miss and total-failure get nothing — the bonus walks.
- A perk grant failure never blocks the job from resolving. You always get your coins/cats.

**How they work:**
- **Timed perks** (e.g. Double Cat, Crate Sniffer) last a few hours.
- **Charge perks** (e.g. Crew Insurance, Heat Shield) hold one or more one-shot uses.
- **Combo perks** (e.g. Combo Shield, Overtime) expire on either timer end OR charge use.
- Granting the same perk again **refreshes** the timer / refills charges — it does NOT stack.
- You can hold up to **5 active perks** at once. A 6th grant drops the oldest *timed* perk; charge-based perks are sticky.

**Pinch immunity:** the Cat Police Pinch suspends *catnip* perks but **not** job perks. Mafia perks were earned, so they keep working through a lockout.

**Reroll Board** is a perk that swaps your current 3 offers (and their previewed perks) for a fresh draw. If active, a yellow button appears on the `/jobs` board next to Help.

Run `/perks` at any time to see your active favors and remaining time/charges.

pages.title: Heat
pages.body:
**Heat** is the cat mafia equivalent of being on the cops' radar. Every job you commit adds heat — **whether you succeed or fail.** Heat doesn't care if the op worked; the mafia made noise.

**Base heat cost by tier** (before multipliers):
```
Tier 1 (Errand)        +0
Tier 2 (Casual)        +8
Tier 3 (Professional) +15
Tier 4 (High-Stakes)  +25
Tier 5 (Big Score)   +100  (auto-pinch on resolve)
```
NPCs apply their own multiplier on top. Jinx: ×0.7 (cheap). Lucian Jr: ×1.15. Sofia: ×1.1. Whiskers: ×0.9. Hostile targets (rep ≤ -75) give you a 25% heat discount.

**Decay:** Heat drops by **2 per hour**, automatically — *but the decay pauses while your catnip session is active*. So running catnip + jobs both at once self-limits via the heat clock.

**Threshold bands:**
```
  0–30   🟢 Clean.    No effect.
 31–70   🟡 Watched.  Heat cost +10%.
 71–99   🔴 Hot.      Heat cost +25%.
   100   🚓 PINCHED.
```

**The Pinch (heat hits 100):**
- The Cat Police catch up with your crew.
- **Catnip perks suspended for 12 hours** (Snowballer, Bait & Switch, pack-drops, double-chance, *all of them*).
- Heat resets to 30 immediately.
- Achievement `feds` unlocks.

The Big Score's +100 heat means **every** Big Score commit pinches you. That's part of the cost.

**How to manage heat:** rotate to a Jinx job (low heat_mult) when you're in the 70+ band, or just stop running jobs for a few hours and let decay catch up. Heat is the brake the system applies to a streaking player; it self-limits without you doing anything.

**Daily commit cap.** Separate from heat: you can only commit a fixed number of jobs (currently 3) per UTC day. The board header shows your `Jobs today: X/3` count; once you hit the cap, the **Send Crew** button refuses with a 'come back tomorrow' message. Three Tier-4 jobs alone are enough to push you into the 70+ heat band, so the cap and heat both act as brakes.

pages.title: The NPCs
pages.body:
Each NPC has a niche. Trade-offs in parentheses are which stat suffers.

- **Whiskers** — reliable, low-heat. Good default.
- **Lucian Jr** — big rewards, but high heat. Low tiers only.
- **Jinx** — very low heat. *(Lower rewards.)*
- **Jeremy** — pays in coins, more of them. *(No cat rewards.)*
- **Lucian Sr** — vendetta jobs against higher-ranked NPCs. Mid-rare cat rewards.
- **Sofia** — best cat hauls at top tiers. *(Higher heat.)*

Bailey, Wilson, and Lalo **never offer jobs** — they exist to be hit.

pages.title: The Big Score
pages.body:
At Lv10, Whiskers offers you the heist of a lifetime: **Wilson's eGirl vault**.

One shot per battlepass season. Difficulty **800 SP** — you'll be putting your rarest cats on the line.

**Reward (success):** 3 eGirls, 15,000 coins, and a permanent **+5% spawn-extra** perk in this server (one-time unlock — subsequent wins don't restack the perk, just give you the loot).

**Near-miss:** half your crew + 5,000 consolation coins.

**Total failure:** the whole crew, gone.

Heat: +100 (auto-pinch). Whether you win or lose, you're getting visited by the Cat Police.

pages.title: Quickstart cheat sheet
pages.body:
1. At Lv2, do your tutorial errand from Lucian Jr.
2. At Lv4, the full board opens. Start with **Whiskers Tier 2** jobs — reliable, low risk.
3. Build Whiskers rep. At +100 he'll start giving you Favors.
4. Watch your heat. If you're at 70+, take a **Jinx** job to cool off.
5. At Lv6+, **Sofia** becomes the best source of mid-rare cats.
6. At Lv10, save up for **The Big Score**. You'll want ~5-6 Ultimates or 4 eGirls minimum.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.body:
- **SP (Send Power)** — per-rarity game-balance number. Distinct from trade value.
- **Ratio** — your crew's total SP divided by the job's difficulty. Feeds the success-chance sigmoid.
- **Near-miss** — narrow 10pp failure band where half your crew survives.
- **Rep** — per-NPC reputation, per-server. Built by working *for* an NPC, tanked by working *against* one.
- **Heat** — accumulating attention from the Cat Police. Decays at 2/hour, paused during catnip.
- **Pinch** — heat hitting 100. Triggers the Cat Police Station event (catnip perks suspended 12h, heat reset to 30).
- **Favor** — Whiskers's +100 unlock. Once per season, your next pack-open upgrades by a tier.
- **The Big Score** — the once-per-season Tier 5 capstone at Lv10.
